title: Nova Locutio DID Document
description: A self-verifying record that publishes key material a did:nova identity cannot embed. A did:nova *is* its Ed25519 signing key, and an X25519 key-agreement key is derived from it by the Edwards->Montgomery map — but a post-quantum ML-KEM key cannot be derived from a curve key, so it must be published. This document binds the did:nova identity to its ML-KEM-768 key-agreement key and is signed by that very identity, so any party can verify it with no central authority (principle 7). The ML-KEM keypair is derived deterministically from the agent's seed, so one seed still regenerates every key. See spec/did-document.md.

id.description: The subject identity: a did:nova:<64-hex> embedding the Ed25519 public key that signs this document.

keyAgreement.description: Published key-agreement keys. At least one ML-KEM-768 key is expected for post-quantum (`kex: x25519-mlkem768`) envelopes.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature (`ed25519:<base64>`) over the JCS-canonical document with the `signature` field removed, verifiable against the Ed25519 key in `id`.

id.description: Key identifier, conventionally the subject DID with a fragment (e.g. '<did>#mlkem768').

type.description: Key-agreement algorithm. v0.3 defines ML-KEM-768 (FIPS 203). A closed enum; new algorithms are additive.

publicKeyBase64.description: The raw algorithm public key, base64 (RFC 4648). For ML-KEM-768 this is the 1184-byte encapsulation key.
